title: Variation on synthesis output based on compiler and compiler flags
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

This replication is intended to be run on linux, but I expect any attempt to compare output from clang-compiled yosys and gcc-compiled yosys will replicate the issue.

1) Untar [yosys_variation.tar.gz](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/3602665/yosys_variation.tar.gz)
2) Run "./make_conda_yosys.sh" to build a gcc and clang compiled version
3) Run "./run_all.sh" to compile example circuit under both versions of yosys

Examples of difference in output:
```
6273c6273
< Extracted 12460 gates and 14102 wires to a netlist network with 1640 inputs and 1387 outputs.
---
> Extracted 12459 gates and 14101 wires to a netlist network with 1640 inputs and 1387 outputs.
```

```
6733,6734c6746,6747
<    Number of wires:               4784
<    Number of wire bits:           8096
---
>    Number of wires:               4776
>    Number of wire bits:           8088
6740c6753
<    Number of cells:               6248
---
>    Number of cells:               6240
6748c6761
<      SB_LUT4                      4607
---
>      SB_LUT4                      4599
```

This issue was original replicated between clang and gcc, but even with the same gcc with different flags results in different outputs.

Known variations:
1) `gcc -fPIC -Os`
2) `clang-7 -fPIC -Os`
3) `clang-8 -fPIC -Os`
4) `gcc -fvisibility-inlines-hidden -fmessage-length=0 -march=nocona -mtune=haswell -ftree-vectorize -fPIC -fstack-protector-strong -fno-plt -O2 -ffunction-sections -fPIC -Os` 

## Expected behavior

Same input files, at the same git commit, outputs the same final circuit.

## Actual behavior

Variation on circuit output of the same input, at the same git commit, is unexpected.
